title: Week of April 6, 2026
summary: This week brought significant improvements to platform stability and performance. OpsPal now automatically detects and fixes common setup issues that previously required manual troubleshooting, while several integrations received enhanced compatibility updates. The platform also added better quality checks to ensure your automations run more reliably.

entries.headline: Enhanced automation quality checks prevent common workflow errors
details: Automations now validate their outputs meet platform standards
Advisory-only workflows are properly distinguished from enforcement workflows
Faster processing when automations detect irrelevant data

entries.headline: Automatic cleanup removes outdated files and improves performance
details: Removes old cached files older than 7 days
Cleans up abandoned session files older than 5 minutes
Warns when memory usage logs become too large

entries.headline: Platform automatically removes broken automation components
details: Works across all operating systems including Windows, Mac, and Linux
Detects and removes non-functional automations from any source
Applies to all installed plugins, not just specific ones

entries.headline: Salesforce integration prevents field and profile configuration errors
details: Detects when Dynamic Forms are active and prevents invisible field issues
Automatically assigns new record types to administrator profiles
Converts blocking routing errors to warnings that won't stop your work

entries.headline: Platform automatically installs missing dependencies and reports issues
details: Checks for missing components when sessions start
Provides clear error messages and fix instructions when problems occur
Automatically attempts to fix safe dependency issues in the background

entries.headline: Resolved false error messages about missing packages at startup
